skill.name: agentdb-memory-patterns
skill.description: Implement persistent memory patterns for AI agents using AgentDB, including session memory, long-term storage, pattern learning, context retrieval, and ReasoningBank migration.
skill.summary: Build persistent AI agent memory with AgentDB patterns and ReasoningBank integration.
skill.author: ruvnet

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified many external command examples and local database paths, but they are presented as documentation for AgentDB usage rather than code that runs automatically. The critical heuristic is dismissed because no prompt injection, credential access, obfuscation, or automatic network exfiltration was found in SKILL.md. Medium risk remains because users are instructed to run npx agentdb@latest and manage persistent memory databases that may contain sensitive conversation data.

medium_findings.title: External Package Commands Require User Trust
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run npx agentdb@latest and related CLI commands. This is legitimate AgentDB setup documentation, but executing a latest-tag npm package can run remote package code with local user permissions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly visible in bash examples and use npx with the latest tag. They are documentation examples, so the risk depends on user execution rather than automatic skill behavior.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Memory Databases May Store Sensitive Data
medium_findings.description: The skill demonstrates storing conversation memory, exporting databases, and migrating legacy memory files. This is expected functionality, but users must protect database and backup files that may contain private agent interactions or user preferences.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file paths and memory operations are explicit, and the skill purpose is persistent agent memory. No unauthorized disclosure is shown, but sensitive data exposure is a realistic operational risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Links Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded GitHub and website URLs are learning resources, not automatic network requests. They do not send local data or credentials from the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only under the Learn More section. No fetch, curl, webhook, or automatic transfer behavior is present at those lines.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Appear To Be False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography hits point to descriptive text and database path examples. No cryptographic algorithm selection or digest function is shown at the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines found skill description text and AgentDB file paths, not MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak cryptographic API usage.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Was Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The analyzer combined command examples, documentation links, and database paths into a critical heuristic. No evidence found for obfuscation, prompt injection, credential access, or automatic data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited behaviors are visible but occur in documentation examples. The file does not contain code that combines credential access with network transfer or hidden execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx latest-tag CLI Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples use npx agentdb@latest, which resolves code from npm at execution time. Users should pin versions and review package provenance before running the commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact npx latest commands are present throughout SKILL.md. This is a known supply-chain risk pattern even when the documentation intent is legitimate.

dangerous_patterns.title: Memory Export And Migration Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The import, export, stats, and migrate examples operate on local database and backup files. These operations can expose stored memory if backups or migrated files are mishandled.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands clearly operate on persistent database files. The skill does not show exfiltration, but the data handling risk follows directly from the memory workflow.

content.value_statement: Stateful agents need reliable memory across sessions, tasks, and user interactions. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical AgentDB patterns for persistent storage, retrieval, learning, and migration.
actual_capabilities: Shows AgentDB initialization patterns for local and in-memory databases.
Demonstrates session memory, long-term facts, and pattern learning workflows.
Explains vector query, import, export, stats, and benchmark CLI operations.
Provides ReasoningBank migration and AgentDB adapter examples.
Lists learning plugin templates and reasoning agent modules.
limitations: Does not include a complete runnable application or test suite.
Requires AgentDB, Node.js, and related packages to be installed separately.
Uses example embeddings and placeholder database objects in several snippets.
Does not define privacy, retention, or encryption policies for stored memories.

use_cases.title: Add memory to a chat assistant
use_cases.description: Use session and long-term memory patterns to preserve user preferences, recent messages, and useful context.

use_cases.title: Migrate legacy reasoning storage
use_cases.description: Follow the ReasoningBank migration pattern to move existing memory into AgentDB-backed storage.

use_cases.title: Prototype learning agent behavior
use_cases.description: Use pattern storage, learning plugins, and reasoning modules to test adaptive agent workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Create a session memory plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a conversational agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AgentDB memory patterns skill to design session memory for my chat assistant. Include stored fields, retrieval flow, and data retention cautions.

prompt_templates.title: Implement long-term preferences
prompt_templates.prompt: Using this skill, outline how to store and retrieve user preferences with AgentDB. Include confidence tracking and when to update facts.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a ReasoningBank migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced migration from existing memory storage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AgentDB ReasoningBank guidance to create a migration checklist from legacy memory.db to AgentDB. Include validation and rollback steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design adaptive pattern learning
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the learning plugin and reasoning module patterns to design an adaptive agent memory system. Cover training cadence, pruning, and evaluation metrics.

output_examples.input: Design memory for a support assistant that remembers user preferences.
output_examples.output: A concise architecture with session history, long-term preference facts, vector retrieval, and safeguards for stored conversation data.

output_examples.input: Help me migrate existing ReasoningBank memory into AgentDB.
output_examples.output: A migration plan that identifies source and destination databases, validates migrated patterns, checks statistics, and preserves a backup.

output_examples.input: Improve retrieval quality for an agent that has too many stored patterns.
output_examples.output: A tuning checklist covering consolidation, relevance thresholds, quantization, caching, and periodic model training.

best_practices: Pin AgentDB package versions before running CLI examples in production environments.
Classify stored memories by sensitivity and define retention rules before export or migration.
Benchmark retrieval quality and latency after enabling quantization, caching, or consolidation.
anti_patterns: Do not store raw secrets, credentials, or regulated data in agent memory without controls.
Do not run latest-tag package commands in production without reviewing package provenance.
Do not treat learned patterns as permanent truth without confidence checks and pruning.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps design persistent memory for AI agents using AgentDB patterns, CLI workflows, and ReasoningBank integration.

faq.question: Does it install AgentDB automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents commands and code patterns that users may choose to run in their own environment.

faq.question: Is this suitable for Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes MCP setup guidance for Claude Code and supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Can it migrate existing ReasoningBank data?
faq.answer: It provides an example migration pattern from a legacy memory database into an AgentDB reasoning database.

faq.question: What security precautions are important?
faq.answer: Pin package versions, review npm package provenance, protect database files, and avoid storing secrets in memory.

faq.question: Does it provide complete production privacy controls?
faq.answer: No. Teams must add their own access controls, encryption, retention policies, and audit processes.
